description: Curated + template-expanded loci for local consumer microarray interpretation.

loci.clinical_note: ADH1B speeds conversion of ethanol to acetaldehyde; pairs with ALDH2 clearance.

AA.phenotype_short: ADH1B His48/His48 fast ethanol-to-acetaldehyde oxidation marker.
AA.takeaway: ADH1B rs1229984 AA — fast ADH1B marker; interpret downstream acetaldehyde exposure with ALDH2 and alcohol intake.
AA.genotype_meaning: Both copies carry the His48 allele commonly associated with faster ADH1B enzyme activity.
AA.phenotype_meaning: Faster first-pass ethanol oxidation can raise acetaldehyde quickly, especially if ALDH2 clearance is reduced.
AA.biology: ADH1B His48 (often ADH1B*2) has substantially higher catalytic activity than Arg48 in biochemical summaries.
AA.monitoring: Educational alcohol-metabolism context only; phenotype, drinking pattern, and ALDH2 status dominate counseling.
AA.ancestry_note: Allele frequency differs sharply by ancestry; 23andMe reports plus-strand genotype.

AG.takeaway: ADH1B rs1229984 AG — intermediate/fast-leaning ethanol oxidation marker; contextual with ALDH2.
AG.genotype_meaning: One His48 allele and one Arg48 allele at this classical ADH1B marker.
AG.phenotype_meaning: Intermediate ethanol-to-acetaldehyde oxidation tendency relative to homozygous states.
AG.biology: Heterozygotes carry a mixture of faster and slower ADH1B isoforms.
AG.monitoring: Pair with clinician if alcohol counselling is clinically relevant.
AG.ancestry_note: Frequencies vary; label carefully for users with mixed ancestry.

GG.takeaway: ADH1B rs1229984 GG — common slower ADH1B marker; interpret alongside ALDH2 and actual tolerance.
GG.genotype_meaning: Both copies carry the Arg48 allele, commonly summarized as the lower-activity ADH1B form.
GG.phenotype_meaning: Slower ethanol-to-acetaldehyde oxidation compared with His48 carriers at this marker.
GG.biology: Arg48 is the ancestral/lower-activity class I ADH1B form in common educational summaries.
GG.monitoring: This does not predict safe alcohol intake; use clinical and behavioral context.
GG.ancestry_note: Confirm plus-strand orientation; interpretation assumes standard 23andMe raw export orientation.

loci.clinical_note: ADH1C variants modulate class I ethanol metabolism together with ADH1B.

TT.phenotype_short: Ile/Ile summarized pattern at ADH1C rs698.
TT.takeaway: ADH1C rs698 TT — Ile/Ile tagging genotype for class I alcohol dehydrogenase nuance.
TT.genotype_meaning: Homozygous T/T at GRCh37 plus-strand rs698, corresponding to Ile350 in common ADH1C transcript notation.
TT.phenotype_meaning: Small effect ethanol handling modifier vs Val349-containing isoforms.
TT.biology: ADH1C expression contributes alongside ADH1B/ADH1A in hepatic ethanol clearance.
TT.monitoring: Combine with behavioural context; genetics is supplemental.
TT.ancestry_note: Interpretation assumes standard 23andMe/reference plus-strand T/C notation for rs698.

CT.takeaway: ADH1C rs698 CT — heterozygous Ile/Val tagging genotype; a modest alcohol-metabolism modifier.
CT.genotype_meaning: Heterozygous T/C at GRCh37 plus-strand rs698, corresponding to Ile350/Val350 notation.
CT.phenotype_meaning: Intermediate enzyme population mixture at this tagging locus.
CT.biology: Coarse microarray tagging; phased star alleles seldom resolved.
CT.monitoring: Interpret next to intake, ALDH2, medications.
CT.ancestry_note: Interpretation assumes standard 23andMe/reference plus-strand T/C notation for rs698.

CC.phenotype_short: Val/Val summarized pattern at ADH1C rs698.
CC.takeaway: ADH1C rs698 CC — Val/Val tagging genotype; modest class I alcohol dehydrogenase modifier.
CC.genotype_meaning: Homozygous C/C at GRCh37 plus-strand rs698, corresponding to Val350 in common ADH1C transcript notation.
CC.phenotype_meaning: Shifts kinetic summary modestly versus Ile/Ile haplotypes.
CC.biology: Downstream acetaldehyde burden still dominates tolerance narrative with ALDH2.
CC.monitoring: Educational layering only.
CC.ancestry_note: Interpretation assumes standard 23andMe/reference plus-strand T/C notation for rs698.

loci.clinical_note: ALDH2 Lys504 dominates acetaldehyde clearance phenotype when present.

GG.phenotype_short: Active ALDH2 at Glu504 in common notation.
GG.takeaway: No ALDH2 Glu504Lys risk allele detected at rs671.
GG.genotype_meaning: Hom reference for Lys504 substitution.
GG.phenotype_meaning: Typical mitochondrial acetaldehyde clearance summarized in clinician education.
GG.biology: ALDH2 clears acetaldehyde to acetate; dysfunction raises acute and chronic risk with alcohol.
GG.monitoring: Follow population alcohol guidance; phenotype still primary.
GG.ancestry_note: Minor Lys allele enrichment in East Asian cohorts.

AG.takeaway: ALDH2 rs671 AG — slower acetaldehyde clearance and flushing pattern often described.
AG.genotype_meaning: One Lys504 allele.
AG.phenotype_meaning: Acetaldehyde peaks higher for a given drink; cancer risk narrative focuses on heavy use.
AG.biology: Dominant-negative tetramer effects described biochemically.
AG.monitoring: Discuss alcohol moderation/avoidance with clinician if relevant.
AG.ancestry_note: Huge frequency gradient by ancestry.

AA.phenotype_short: Homozygous Lys504 substitution — near loss-of-function summarized.
AA.takeaway: ALDH2 rs671 AA — strong alcohol intolerance biochemical pattern; clinician counselling recommended.
AA.genotype_meaning: Likely Lys/Lys mitochondrial ALDH2 at common missense tagging SNP.
AA.phenotype_meaning: Profound intolerance symptoms with ethanol in many carriers; synergy with tobacco/alcohol oncology risk narratives.
AA.biology: Strong loss-of-function classically summarized as “severe inactivity.”
AA.monitoring: High-priority behavioural counselling; genotype not deterministic for every individual phenotype.
AA.ancestry_note: Verify chip strand; phenotype still gold standard.
